mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound signals sustained monetary accommodation that lowers the equity discount rate and supports risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the low-rate regime persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.07 at snapshot indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant excess return versus higher-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it signals labor market deterioration severe enough to trigger recession-risk repricing by equity investors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 5 trading days as markets digest the confirmed weakening.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.6 at snapshot, above the 6.5% threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE first crosses above 6.5%, or the effect is not distinguishable from random walk baseline at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative federal funds rate jointly indicate that consumer demand is being reinforced by cheap financing, amplifying the positive equity response beyond what either series alone predicts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing with consumer demand expansion
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-growth/low-FEDFUNDS signal fails to outperform a model using RSAFS alone by more than 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth above 2% annualized during a mid-cycle expansion signals durable corporate earnings momentum from the supply side, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only while the expansion regime is intact.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO growth triggers above 2% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during at least two distinct expansion periods, or the effect reverses sign during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth reflects continuous labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on an ongoing basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year window, or negative PAYEMS-change months show equal or higher average forward returns than positive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1000 thousand units signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the housing slowdown propagates through the broader economy.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=999.0 at snapshot, just below the 1000 threshold
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST breaches below 1000 are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, rising CPI inflation combined with a federal funds rate near zero indicates that real interest rates are becoming increasingly negative, boosting near-term equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, an effect specific to ZLB conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.07 at snapshot indicates zero lower bound
failure_condition: The joint CPI-rising/near-zero-FEDFUNDS signal produces 5-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or negative real-rate periods outside ZLB show the same effect, invalidating regime specificity.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated unemployment (UNRATE above 6%) and declining retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signals a demand-labor feedback loop where weak consumption reinforces layoffs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days that exceed what either indicator alone would predict.
failure_condition: The joint high-UNRATE/falling-RSAFS signal's 60-day forward return effect is not more negative than the UNRATE-only signal by at least 0.02 Rank IC, indicating the interaction adds no explanatory power beyond the single-series effect.
